heading: Cybersecurity recruitment and retention
label: 658 Cybersecurity recruitment and retention
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to renumbering by .section 147 of this titlePub. L. 115–278

Change of NameReference to National Protection and Programs Directorate of the Department of Homeland Security deemed to be a reference to the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency of the Department, see , enacted .section 652(a)(2) of this titleNov. 16, 2018

children.heading: Appropriate committees of Congress
children.label: 1 Appropriate committees of Congress
children.text: The term “appropriate committees of Congress” means the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Homeland Security and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Collective bargaining agreement
children.label: 2 Collective bargaining agreement
children.text: The term “collective bargaining agreement” has the meaning given that term in .section 7103(a)(8) of title 5

children.heading: Excepted service
children.label: 3 Excepted service
children.text: The term “excepted service” has the meaning given that term in .section 2103 of title 5

children.heading: Preference eligible
children.label: 4 Preference eligible
children.text: The term “preference eligible” has the meaning given that term in .section 2108 of title 5

children.heading: Qualified position
children.label: 5 Qualified position
children.text: The term “qualified position” means a position, designated by the Secretary for the purpose of this section, in which the incumbent performs, manages, or supervises functions that execute the responsibilities of the Department relating to cybersecurity.

children.heading: Senior Executive Service
children.label: 6 Senior Executive Service
children.text: The term “Senior Executive Service” has the meaning given that term in .section 2101a of title 5

children.heading: Establish positions, appoint personnel, and fix rates of pay
children.label: 1 Establish positions, appoint personnel, and fix rates of pay
I
children.text: senior level positions designated under ; andsection 5376 of title 5

children.label: II
children.text: positions in the Senior Executive Service;

children.label: ii
children.text: appoint an individual to a qualified position (after taking into consideration the availability of preference eligibles for appointment to the position); and

children.label: iii
children.text: subject to the requirements of paragraphs (2) and (3), fix the compensation of an individual for service in a qualified position.

children.heading: Construction with other laws
children.label: B Construction with other laws
children.text: The authority of the Secretary under this subsection applies without regard to the provisions of any other law relating to the appointment, number, classification, or compensation of employees.

children.heading: Authority to fix rates of basic pay
children.label: A Authority to fix rates of basic pay
children.text: In accordance with this section, the Secretary shall fix the rates of basic pay for any qualified position established under paragraph (1) in relation to the rates of pay provided for employees in comparable positions in the Department of Defense and subject to the same limitations on maximum rates of pay established for such employees by law or regulation.

children.heading: Prevailing rate systems
children.label: B Prevailing rate systems
children.text: The Secretary may, consistent with , adopt such provisions of that title as provide for prevailing rate systems of basic pay and may apply those provisions to qualified positions for employees in or under which the Department may employ individuals described by section 5342(a)(2)(A) of that title.section 5341 of title 5

children.heading: Additional compensation based on title 5 authorities
children.label: A Additional compensation based on title 5 authorities
children.text: The Secretary may provide employees in qualified positions compensation (in addition to basic pay), including benefits, incentives, and allowances, consistent with, and not in excess of the level authorized for, comparable positions authorized by title 5.

children.heading: Allowances in nonforeign areas
children.label: B Allowances in nonforeign areas
children.text: An employee in a qualified position whose rate of basic pay is fixed under paragraph (2)(A) shall be eligible for an allowance under , on the same basis and to the same extent as if the employee was an employee covered by such section 5941, including eligibility conditions, allowance rates, and all other terms and conditions in law or regulation.section 5941 of title 5

children.heading: Plan for execution of authorities
children.label: 4 Plan for execution of authorities
children.text: Not later than 120 days after , the Secretary shall submit a report to the appropriate committees of Congress with a plan for the use of the authorities provided under this subsection.December 18, 2014

children.heading: Collective bargaining agreements
children.label: 5 Collective bargaining agreements
children.text: Nothing in paragraph (1) may be construed to impair the continued effectiveness of a collective bargaining agreement with respect to an office, component, subcomponent, or equivalent of the Department that is a successor to an office, component, subcomponent, or equivalent of the Department covered by the agreement before the succession.

children.heading: Required regulations
children.label: 6 Required regulations
children.text: The Secretary, in coordination with the Director of the Office of Personnel Management, shall prescribe regulations for the administration of this section.

discusses the process used by the Secretary in accepting applications, assessing candidates, ensuring adherence to veterans’ preference, and selecting applicants for vacancies to be filled by an individual for a qualified position;

children.label: A
children.text: how the Secretary plans to fulfill the critical need of the Department to recruit and retain employees in qualified positions;

children.label: B
children.text: the measures that will be used to measure progress; and

children.label: C
children.text: any actions taken during the reporting period to fulfill such critical need;
discusses how the planning and actions taken under paragraph (2) are integrated into the strategic workforce planning of the Department;

children.label: A
children.text: the number of employees in qualified positions hired by occupation and grade and level or pay band;

children.label: B
children.text: the placement of employees in qualified positions by directorate and office within the Department;

children.label: C
children.text: the total number of veterans hired;

children.label: D
children.text: the number of separations of employees in qualified positions by occupation and grade and level or pay band;

children.label: E
children.text: the number of retirements of employees in qualified positions by occupation and grade and level or pay band; and

children.label: F
children.text: the number and amounts of recruitment, relocation, and retention incentives paid to employees in qualified positions by occupation and grade and level or pay band; and
describes the training provided to supervisors of employees in qualified positions at the Department on the use of the new authorities.

children.heading: Three-year probationary period
children.label: d Three-year probationary period
children.text: The probationary period for all employees hired under the authority established in this section shall be 3 years.

children.heading: Incumbents of existing competitive service positions
children.label: e Incumbents of existing competitive service positions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An individual serving in a position on , that is selected to be converted to a position in the excepted service under this section shall have the right to refuse such conversion.December 18, 2014

children.heading: Subsequent conversion
children.label: 2 Subsequent conversion
children.text: After the date on which an individual who refuses a conversion under paragraph (1) stops serving in the position selected to be converted, the position may be converted to a position in the excepted service.
the Subcommittee on Homeland Security of the Committee on Appropriations and the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate; and
the Subcommittee on Homeland Security of the Committee on Appropriations and the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives.
